QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_20

question.text: The term "substantial interest" for the purpose of clubbing of income under Section 64(1)(ii) means holding beneficial ownership of at least what percentage of voting power or profit share in a concern?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 64(1)(ii), "substantial interest" is defined as holding at least 20% of the voting power (in a company) or at least 20% share in the profits (in a non-corporate entity like a partnership firm) at any time during the previous year. **The Trap:** 26% is the threshold for "special resolution blocking" under Companies Act (different context). 10% is used in some other tax provisions. 51% is majority control. The specific 20% threshold for clubbing purposes must be remembered precisely. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A and X-B both test the 20% threshold. It is also relevant in the context of related party transactions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When a client's spouse works in a family business, immediately check if the client holds 20%+ — if yes, the spouse's salary may be clubbed unless the spouse has qualifying professional credentials.
